slides.title: Be kinder to yourself when you *fall short*.
slides.sub: How you respond to your own setbacks shapes your body's stress response. Here is the science of self-compassion.

slides.title: Self-criticism is a stressor
slides.body: Harsh self-talk reads to the body like a *real threat*. The same alarm system that fires for danger fires for a missed goal.

slides.title: Kindness calms the alarm
slides.body: Meeting a setback with warmth instead of blame *can lower cortisol* and slow the heart, signaling that you are safe.

slides.title: Chronic stress adds up
slides.body: A stress response that never fully switches off *is linked to* faster aging, in the heart and across the body.

slides.title: Talk to yourself like a friend
slides.body: Notice the moment you slip, name it plainly, and respond the way you would to *someone you love*.

slides.title: It builds with reps
slides.body: Self-compassion is a skill, not a mood. Small daily choices to ease up *may help* steady your baseline over time.

slides.title: Track the calm, not the *guilt*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so an off day is data to learn from, not a reason to pile on.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The way you talk to yourself after a setback is doing something physical. Kindness can settle your heart rate and stress hormones. 🌿

Self-criticism keeps the alarm system on, while self-compassion helps it switch off and eases the long wear of chronic stress. Save this for a hard day. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your habits into one calm daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Self-Compassion Lowers Stress and Protects Your Heart
